license_note: Government public information page; verify reuse before redistribution.
publisher: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of India
text_preview: The programme covers veterinary infrastructure, disease surveillance, mobile veterinary units, and call centre support. It is useful for learning Indian animal-health system language and escalation context.
title: Livestock Health and Disease Control Programme

license_note: Government public information page; verify reuse before redistribution.
publisher: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of India
text_preview: NADCP describes FMD and brucellosis control, outbreak investigation, and regulation of animal movement. It is high-value India anchor text for reportable disease vocabulary.
title: National Animal Disease Control Programme

license_note: NDDB public PDF; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: This handbook covers dairy animal management, housing, feeding, health practices, calf care, and clean milk production. It anchors Indian dairy extension language for CPT.
title: Handbook of Good Dairy Husbandry Practices

license_note: NDDB public farmer page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Dairy animals need regular access to clean drinking water. Water support is especially important in summer and for milk production, making this useful routine-care CPT text.
title: Importance of Drinking Water for Dairy Animals

license_note: NDDB public farmer page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Young calves should be reared carefully with attention to feeding, clean practices, growth, and health. This gives farmer-facing calf-care vocabulary.
title: Calf Nutrition

license_note: NDDB public service page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Clean milk production depends on hygiene, clean utensils, and careful handling at village level. It is useful for milk hygiene language without certifying sale safety.
title: Clean Milk Production Awareness

license_note: NDDB public farmer page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Bloat involves gas accumulation in the rumen and visible belly swelling. Prevention context includes careful feeding and avoiding risky grazing patterns. Do not include invasive treatment instructions in CPT chunks.
title: Bloat

license_note: NDDB public farmer page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Mold on damp feed can produce toxins and reduce animal health and production. This text supports feed-storage and spoiled-feed vocabulary for CPT.
title: Aflatoxicosis

license_note: NDDB public farmer page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Regular disposal of manure and urine and avoiding stagnant drainage around cattle sheds helps reduce flies and maintain cleaner animal surroundings.
title: Fly Control

license_note: University extension page; verify reuse before redistribution.
publisher: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University Agritech Portal
text_preview: Calving and calf management text covers clean calving areas, bedding, newborn care, and basic management around birth. It is useful for Indian extension terminology.
title: Calf Management

license_note: FAO public manual; check FAO reuse terms before redistribution.
text_preview: Clean housing improves cow and calf health and milk quality. The manual covers manure handling, clean feeders and drinkers, insect control, safe floors, ventilation, and environmental comfort.
title: Small-Scale Dairy Farming Manual: Dairy Cattle and Buffalo Housing

license_note: FAO public manual; check FAO reuse terms before redistribution.
text_preview: Dairy cattle and buffalo need nutrients from roughages, concentrates, minerals, vitamins, and water. The manual explains digestion, feed types, feed value, and productivity context.
title: Small-Scale Dairy Farming Manual: Feeding Dairy Cattle and Buffalo

license_note: FAO public manual; check FAO reuse terms before redistribution.
text_preview: Disease is described as a change that can lower production or cause death. The manual introduces common condition categories and disease observation language without needing raw treatment recipes.
title: Small-Scale Dairy Farming Manual: Conditions Affecting Dairy Cattle and Buffalo

license_note: FAO public manual; check FAO reuse terms before redistribution.
text_preview: Parasites can reduce production and affect young animals strongly. This source is useful for broad parasite vocabulary and observation language, not farmer dosing instructions.
title: Small-Scale Dairy Farming Manual: Parasites in Dairy Cattle and Buffalo

license_note: FAO/IDF publication; check reuse terms before redistribution.
text_preview: Good dairy farming practice covers animal health, milk hygiene, nutrition, welfare, environment, and socio-economic management in practical farmer-oriented language.
title: Guide to Good Dairy Farming Practice

license_note: WOAH standards page; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: WOAH standards provide high-level vocabulary for animal welfare, handling, housing, and production systems. Country-specific legal language should remain metadata, not farmer advice.
title: Animal Welfare and Dairy Cattle Production Systems

license_note: Reference text; verify license and keep only non-procedural snippets for CPT.
text_preview: Veterinary reference material can improve disease terminology and clinical-sign vocabulary. Sections with drug doses, routes, surgery, and procedures must be quarantined or stripped.
title: MSD Veterinary Manual: Cattle Health Topics

license_note: University extension content; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Dairy extension articles cover practical herd management, calf care, nutrition, housing, recordkeeping, and farm workflows. Highly local regulations should be labeled, not used as India advice.
title: Dairy Extension Articles

license_note: University extension content; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: University dairy extension resources provide broad management, feeding, housing, calf, and health vocabulary that can help CPT domain familiarity.
title: Dairy Extension Resources

license_note: Secondary public information portal; verify reuse before redistribution.
text_preview: Useful as farmer-facing context and language exposure. Do not use as sole factual grounding for safety-critical SFT/RAG claims.
title: Dairy Animal Management Context
